specific_mechanism: ergodic decomposition (Birkhoff-Khinchin decomposition of an invariant measure into ergodic components)
llm_application: Empirical ergodic-component identification on long-running LLM-agent (state, action) trajectories: cluster observed sub-trajectories into ergodic invariant subsets, then operationalize 'capability mode transitions' and 'policy lock-in' as crossings (or failure to cross) between ergodic components. The evaluation reports the empirical ergodic decomposition itself as the metric, instead of a benchmark snapshot.
novelty_claim: Existing longitudinal agent evaluation either treats trajectories as cost-to-reduce or measures aggregate drift in goal/belief space. No published work applies the ergodic decomposition theorem to long agent trajectories to formalize 'the agent is stuck in invariant component C' as the failure mode, with Birkhoff time-average estimators serving as the test statistic for component identity.
